metadata.notes: Content overhaul — Wingspan teaching standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, Dized interactive rules, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Mats
- 1 Basecamp board (central display with card slots and glory track)
- 20 hexagonal location tiles in 3 regions: 6 South, 7 Central, 7 North
- 1 mech mat per player (tracks power, guile, workers, and the action token)

#### Cards
- 123 uniquely illustrated cards in a single shuffled deck, comprising 3 types:
  - **Item cards** — have an instant benefit and an ongoing ability; can be upgraded
  - **Meteorite cards** — have a meld ability; tuck under your mech mat for cascading bonuses
  - **Quest cards** — have a solve ability; require your mech at the matching location
- 1 character card + 1 companion card per player (starting hand)

#### Miniatures & Tokens
- 1 large mech miniature per player (snaps onto silicone base)
- 4 glory tokens (stars) per player
- 1 action token (cube) per player
- Power and guile tracking tokens per player
- 50 worker meeples in 5 colors
- 36 corruption tiles + 1 cloth bag
- Map tokens, coins (limited supply)

#### Other Components
- 14 Automa cards (solo mode)
- 2 player aid cards per player
- Custom insert with setup tray

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Overview
Expeditions is a standalone sequel to Scythe, set in an alternate 1920s. A meteorite has crashed near the Tunguska River, awakening ancient corruption. Players pilot mechs into the wilderness to explore locations, play cards to build engines, gather resources, and vanquish corruption.

#### The Action Token System
Your mech mat has 3 action spaces — **Move**, **Play**, and **Gather** — plus a **Refresh** space. The action token covers one space; you perform the uncovered actions.

**After a Refresh**: Action token sits on Refresh. Shift it left — all 3 actions (Move, Play, Gather) are uncovered. Perform all 3 in any order.

**Subsequent turns**: Move the action token into the Move/Play/Gather area, covering exactly 1 action. Perform the other 2 in any order. Each turn, shift the token to cover a different action.

**Refresh turn**: Instead of normal actions, return all active row cards to your hand and all workers to your mat. Place the action token on Refresh. Next turn you get all 3 actions again.

#### The Card Engine
Cards move between 3 zones:
- **Hand** (left of mech mat) — cards you can play
- **Active row** (right of mech mat) — cards in play, generating abilities
- **Tucked** (under mech mat edges) — upgraded items, melded meteorites, solved quests (permanent scoring)

#### Game End
When any player places their **4th glory token** via Boast, the endgame triggers. Every player (including the triggering player) takes exactly 1 final turn. Most money wins.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
1. Place Basecamp board centrally. Arrange the 6 South location tiles face-up per the layout diagram.
2. Shuffle Central (7) and North (7) tiles face-down in their spots. Place 1 map token on each face-down tile.
3. Shuffle all item, meteorite, and quest cards into one deck. Deal 5 cards face-up onto the Basecamp display.
4. Fill the corruption bag with all tokens except the 20-value token (set aside for Location 20).
5. Each player takes: mech mat + mech miniature, character card + companion card (into hand), 4 glory tokens, 1 action token (on Refresh space), power/guile tokens at zero.
6. Place all mechs adjacent to Basecamp. Randomly select first player.

#### The Four Actions

**MOVE** — Move your mech 1-3 spaces (Marsh Strider: 1-4). You cannot end on an occupied space but may move through them. You cannot return to Basecamp after your first move.
- **Revealing tiles**: When you move onto a face-down tile, movement ends immediately. Gain the map token on it. Flip the tile face-up. Draw corruption tokens from the bag until their total value meets or exceeds the location's printed value — stack them on the location.

**PLAY** — Play exactly 1 card from hand to the rightmost position in your active row. Then in any order:
- Gain the **core value** (number in upper-left) — advance power or guile (max 10).
- **Activate the ability** — place a matching-color worker from your mat onto the card. Items have instant + ongoing benefits. Quests have a Solve ability. Meteorites have a Meld ability.
- Worker color must match the card's requirement. Max 1 worker per card. Each card's ability activates at most once per turn.

**GATHER** — Gain all exposed benefits at your mech's current location. Corruption tokens cover benefits until vanquished. You must gain all exposed benefits if possible.
- Benefits include: gain a face-up card, sweep the display, gain a worker, gain a map token, play a card, upgrade an item, meld a meteorite, boast, and others.

**REFRESH** — Return all active row cards to hand and all workers to mat. Place action token on Refresh. Next turn: all 3 actions.

#### Vanquish (via card abilities, not a standalone action)
- Pay the cost on the topmost corruption token at your mech's location using power and/or guile.
- Take the token — it scores $2 at game end.
- May repeat to vanquish additional tokens at the same location.
- When the final corruption is removed, the hidden benefit underneath becomes available.
- **Location 20**: Requires BOTH 10 power AND 10 guile (not either/or). Still only worth $2.

#### Solve (Quests)
- Mech must be at the location matching the quest card.
- Pay the power/guile cost shown. Gain the benefit.
- Tuck the quest under the top edge of your mech mat. Max 4 solved quests.

#### Upgrade (Items)
- Tuck an item from hand or active row under the right edge of your mech mat.
- The ongoing ability becomes permanently active (no worker needed).
- Max 4 upgraded items. The coin value on the item scores at game end.

#### Meld (Meteorites)
- Tuck a meteorite under the bottom edge of your mech mat.
- Immediately gain the meld bonus of ALL currently melded meteorites (cascading). Each successive meld is more powerful.
- Max 4 melded meteorites.

#### Boast (Placing Glory Stars)
- Choose 1 glory category on the Basecamp board that you've achieved.
- Place your glory star. Multiple players can have stars on the same category.
- 7 categories: Quests (4 solved), Meteorites (4 melded), Items (4 upgraded), Location 20 (vanquished), Corruption (7+ tokens), Cards (8+ controlled), Workers/Maps (7+ workers OR 5+ map tokens).
- You need only 4 of 7 categories to trigger endgame.

#### Mech Abilities
- **Tatanka**: 5 slots for quests/melds/upgrades (instead of 4)
- **Marsh Strider**: Movement range 1-4 (instead of 1-3)
- **Lumberjack**: Reduce first corruption cost per color by 1
- **Highlander**: Gain cards to hand instead of active row
- **Odin's Wrath**: Trash a map token for 1 power, 1 guile, or $1

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End Trigger
When any player places their **4th glory token** via Boast, the endgame triggers. Every player (including the triggering player) takes exactly **1 final turn**.

#### Final Scoring (All Money)
All scoring converts to money. Most money wins.

| Source | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Coins | Face value |
| Upgraded items | Coin value printed on card |
| Corruption tokens | $2 each (including the 20-value token) |
| Glory stars | Variable — scales with quests solved |

**Glory Star Value by Quests Solved:**
- 0 quests: $5 per star
- 1 quest: $6 per star
- 2 quests: $8 per star
- 3+ quests: **$10 per star**

With 4 stars and 3 solved quests, stars alone = $40. With 0 quests, stars = $20. Solving quests is the single biggest scoring multiplier.

#### Tiebreaker
1. Sum of all glory category values (totals across every category, including those without stars).
2. If still tied: shared victory.

#### Typical Score Ranges
- First game: $35-50
- Experienced play: $55-75
- Highly optimized: $80+

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Minimize Refresh Turns
Refreshing is essentially a lost turn — you produce nothing. The best players time refreshes to extract maximum value from each Move/Play/Gather cycle. Every unnecessary Refresh is a turn your opponents are advancing.

#### Solve 3 Quests as a Priority
This is the single biggest scoring multiplier in the game. It raises each glory star from $5 to $10 — meaning 4 stars go from $20 total to $40 total, a $20 swing. Even solving 2 quests ($8/star) is a significant improvement over 0.

#### Plan Multiple Turns Ahead
Solving a quest requires 4 conditions simultaneously: (a) you control a quest card, (b) your mech is at the matching location, (c) you have enough power/guile, (d) you have a card with a Solve ability to play. This requires careful multi-turn setup.

#### Meld Meteorites for Cascading Value
Each time you meld, you gain the meld bonus of ALL previously melded meteorites plus the new one. The 4th meld triggers all 4 bonuses at once. Melding gets progressively more powerful.

#### Leverage Your Mech's Ability
- **Tatanka**: Go wide — 5 slots for quests/melds/upgrades.
- **Marsh Strider**: Extra movement range for positional advantage.
- **Highlander**: Cards to hand gives flexibility on timing.
- **Lumberjack**: Lean into vanquishing corruption with discount costs.
- **Odin's Wrath**: Convert excess map tokens into flexible resources.

#### Choose Your 4 Glory Paths Adaptively
You need only 4 of 7 categories. Read the board state, your cards, and opponents' progress. Pick the 4 categories most efficient for your current engine.

#### Watch the Endgame Trigger
The game ends when anyone places their 4th star. Be aware of opponents approaching that threshold — you may need to accelerate or trigger the end at an advantageous moment.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Refreshing too often** — Every Refresh is a lost turn. Build bigger active rows before resetting.
- **Ignoring quests** — 0 solved quests means your 4 stars are worth only $20 instead of $40. Quests are not optional.
- **Spreading too thin** — Focus on 4 achievable glory categories rather than chasing all 7.
- **Forgetting worker color matching** — Workers must match the card's color requirement.
- **Not gathering all exposed benefits** — You MUST gain all exposed benefits when Gathering. Plan your position accordingly.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Expeditions

**Setup**: You start with your mech at Basecamp, a character card and companion card in hand, action token on Refresh, and power/guile at zero. The 6 South locations are face-up; Central and North tiles are face-down with map tokens.

**Turn 1 (All 3 actions)**: Your action token is on Refresh, so shift it left — you get Move, Play, and Gather. Move your mech to a nearby South location. Play your character card to your active row — gain its core value (advance power or guile) and activate its ability by placing a matching worker. Gather at your location to pick up an exposed benefit (maybe a face-up card or a worker).

**Turn 2 (2 actions)**: Shift your action token to cover one of the three actions. If you cover Move, you stay put and Play + Gather. If you cover Gather, you Move + Play. Choose based on what benefits your current location and hand.

**Turns 3-5**: Build your active row with item and meteorite cards. Move to face-down tiles to explore — flip them, gain map tokens, and spawn corruption. Start building power and guile toward vanquishing corruption or solving quests.

**Mid-game**: Your active row should have 3-4 cards generating abilities. When it feels cluttered or your workers are all placed, take a Refresh turn — return everything to hand and reset. Next turn you get all 3 actions again.

**Quest Solving**: When you have a quest card, position your mech at the matching location, build enough power/guile, and play a card with a Solve ability. The quest tucks under your mat permanently. Aim for at least 2-3 solved quests — the scoring multiplier is enormous.

**Endgame Push**: Once you've completed enough for 4 glory categories (e.g., 4 solved quests, 4 upgraded items, 7+ corruption tokens, 8+ cards controlled), find Boast benefits and place your 4 stars. The game ends after everyone takes 1 final turn.

**Scoring**: Add up coins + upgraded item values + $2 per corruption token + glory star values (scaled by quests solved). First game target: $40-55 is solid.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Map Control

Expeditions is about exploring the board efficiently. **Key insight**: tiles closer to base camp are safer but lower value. Push deep for high-value tiles, but plan your return path — you score nothing if you can't get items back.

#### Card Engine Priorities

Your mech's upgrade cards define your strategy. **Movement upgrades** first (more efficient exploration), then **combat** (to claim contested tiles), then **resource generation** (for the end-game push). Don't upgrade evenly — specialize.

#### Worker Placement Timing

Workers on tiles claim them. Place workers early on contested tiles (near multiple players), but delay placement on remote tiles that only you can reach. Workers are limited — every placement is a commitment.

#### Corruption Management

Corruption cards clog your hand but are sometimes unavoidable. Spend actions to remove corruption before it accumulates. Two corruption cards reduce your effective hand size significantly. Prevention is better than cure — avoid actions that generate corruption when possible.

#### End-Game Trigger Awareness

The game ends when someone places their final worker or the tile stack depletes. Track both conditions. If you're ahead, accelerate; if behind, delay by exploring new tiles instead of claiming.

rulings: The action token stays in the Move/Play/Gather box once placed there. You must Refresh to return to all-3-actions mode.
'Gain 1 location benefit' means exactly 1 benefit icon, even if the location shows multiple icons.
A card's ability may only activate once per turn unless the card specifically states otherwise.
Adjacency is always relative to the mech's physical position on the map, not to cards in your active row.
'Gain the core value of all solved quests' includes the quest being solved in the same action.
You must gain ALL exposed benefits when Gathering if possible. You cannot selectively skip them.
Power and guile cap at 10. Effects that would push above 10 stop at the cap.
The 20-value corruption requires BOTH 10 power AND 10 guile (not either/or). It is still only worth $2 at game end.
Worker color must match the card's color requirement to activate an ability.
Gaining a Refresh benefit from a card is NOT the same as taking the Refresh action — your action token does NOT move to the Refresh space.
